meta.name: Operation PACIFIC SENTINEL
meta.description: Notional corps operation in the Indo-Pacific across 96 hours. Demonstrates phase-aware criticality: the Phase I fiber trunk cut and backup relay strike converge corps reach-back onto a single shared ground station in Phase II.
assumptions: All units, systems, locations, links, events, and timings in this scenario are entirely fictional and were generated solely for product demonstration.
Real basing and geographic names are used only to make the map read plausibly; no real-world force disposition, capability, or plan is represented.
H-hours are exercise-relative: H+0 is scenario start, not any real-world date or event.
Scenario themes drawn from unclassified open-source wargaming (e.g., CSIS 'First Battle of the Next War' 2023; RAND Indo-Pacific analyses); all units, systems, and events are fictional.

phases.label: Set Conditions
phases.notes: Shaping and counter-ISR; sustainment flows uncontested on fiber and SATCOM; adversary intrusions probe port and logistics networks.

phases.label: Close Operations
phases.main_effort: I Corps (FWD) deep fires — LRPF kill chain vs OPFOR ASCM and IADS
phases.notes: Missile salvos strike theater airbases; the Palawan trunk is cut and Relay WHISKEY struck — SATCOM through Ground Station KILO becomes the single thread, and dispersal and magazine depth decide the fight.

phases.label: Consolidate
phases.notes: Sealift surge and runway repair; degraded C2 paths restored; risk shifts to sustainment endurance and interceptor stocks.

nodes.name: I Corps (FWD) Main Command Post
nodes.mission: Provides corps-level mission command and synchronizes operations across all warfighting functions for Operation PACIFIC SENTINEL.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Main CP, COP servers, and co-located data systems draw from a single prime power generator farm.

nodes.name: I Corps (FWD) Tactical Command Post
nodes.mission: Forward mission command node that controls the close fight during Phase II close operations.

nodes.name: 25th ID (Slice) Division Headquarters
nodes.mission: Commands the division slice executing close operations in northern Luzon.

nodes.name: 25th ID JAGIC
nodes.mission: Integrates joint air-ground fires and airspace control for the division close area.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Joint data feeds ride the Palawan fiber trunk in Phase I and reroute to SATCOM after H+36.

nodes.name: I Corps (FWD) Fires Cell
nodes.mission: Plans and controls corps deep fires and manages the LRPF and MLRS kill chains.

nodes.name: 17th Sustainment Brigade HQ
nodes.mission: Directs corps sustainment operations through the Subic seaport and Clark aerial port.

nodes.name: Btry A, 1-181 Long-Range Fires Battalion (LRPF)
nodes.mission: Delivers long-range precision fires against OPFOR anti-ship missile and air defense targets.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Fire mission data reach-back rides SATCOM through Ground Station KILO after the Phase I fiber trunk is cut.

nodes.name: Task Force Talon (Aviation)
nodes.mission: Conducts attack and assault aviation operations in support of division close operations.

nodes.name: 2-95 Field Artillery (MLRS)
nodes.mission: Provides rocket and missile fires against OPFOR air defense and C2 targets in the deep area.

nodes.name: SATCOM Gateway ECHO
nodes.mission: Aggregates corps wideband SATCOM reach-back for mission command, fires, intelligence, and sustainment traffic.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Phase II reroute concentrates fires, sustainment, and JAGIC traffic on this single gateway path.

nodes.name: WGS-11 (Wideband SATCOM)
nodes.mission: Provides primary wideband SATCOM transport between the corps gateway and Ground Station KILO.

nodes.name: WGS-6 (Wideband SATCOM)
nodes.mission: Provides alternate wideband SATCOM transport supporting the backup relay path.

nodes.name: Ground Station KILO
nodes.mission: Regional wideband teleport that terminates corps SATCOM reach-back and passes it to the theater network.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Becomes the sole reach-back teleport for the corps once the Palawan trunk is cut (H+36) and Relay Site WHISKEY is struck (H+40) — a Phase II single point of failure that does not exist in Phase I.

nodes.name: Relay Site WHISKEY
nodes.mission: Backup wideband relay providing an alternate reach-back path for the corps gateway.
nodes.notes: Struck by OPFOR ballistic missile at H+40; partial capability only for the remainder of the operation.

nodes.name: Fiber Node PALAWAN
nodes.mission: Undersea fiber trunk landing that carries bulk corps data to the theater network in Phase I.
nodes.notes: Undersea trunk cut by adversary action at H+36; cable-ship repair operation begins in Phase III.

nodes.name: Theater Network Node GUAM
nodes.mission: Theater network point of presence that connects corps reach-back into strategic transport.

nodes.name: Corps Tactical Mesh Node
nodes.mission: Line-of-sight mesh backbone linking the corps TAC CP into the Main CP network.

nodes.name: OTH Radar Detachment PALAU
nodes.mission: Provides wide-area over-the-horizon surveillance of South China Sea surface traffic.

nodes.name: Corps UAS Detachment (Gray Eagle)
nodes.mission: Flies medium-altitude UAS orbits providing full-motion video and target development to the JAGIC.

nodes.name: Maritime Patrol Squadron DET (P-8)
nodes.mission: Conducts maritime patrol and reconnaissance of the Bashi Channel and Luzon Strait approaches.

nodes.name: Corps SIGINT Collection Element
nodes.mission: Collects and reports OPFOR emissions to cue targeting and electronic attack.

nodes.name: Port Logistics Node SUBIC
nodes.mission: Receives and stages seaborne sustainment at Subic Bay for onward movement to the corps.

nodes.name: Airfield Logistics Node CLARK (APOD)
nodes.mission: Operates the aerial port of debarkation at Clark for time-sensitive sustainment and aviation support.

nodes.name: Ammunition Transfer Point TARLAC
nodes.mission: Forward ammunition transfer point issuing Class V to corps fires units.

nodes.name: Sustainment C2 System
nodes.mission: Logistics common operating picture that tracks stocks, movements, and commodity status for the corps.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Reach-back rides the Palawan fiber trunk in Phase I and reroutes to SATCOM through Ground Station KILO after H+36.

nodes.name: 1-62 ADA Battalion (Patriot)
nodes.mission: Provides air and missile defense of the corps Main CP and the SATCOM gateway site.

nodes.name: C-UAS Battery, 1-62 ADA
nodes.mission: Defends the Subic port complex against OPFOR small UAS.

nodes.name: Corps EW Company
nodes.mission: Conducts electronic attack against OPFOR early-warning and coastal surveillance radars.

nodes.name: Corps Cyber Effects Planning Cell
nodes.mission: Plans and coordinates offensive cyber effects against OPFOR IADS command and control.

nodes.name: Mission Command Platform (Corps COP)
nodes.mission: Hosts the corps common operational picture and mission command services for all CPs.

nodes.name: Fires Data System
nodes.mission: Processes fire missions and technical fire direction for LRPF and MLRS units.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Depends on GPS timing for synchronization; degraded GPS slows the Phase II kill chain.

nodes.name: Logistics Data System
nodes.mission: Aggregates supply, maintenance, and distribution data feeding the sustainment and corps common operating pictures.

nodes.name: Corps Intelligence Database
nodes.mission: Stores fused all-source intelligence and target materials serving the JAGIC, fires, and the COP.

nodes.name: OPFOR Regional C2 Node
nodes.mission: Directs OPFOR anti-access operations, integrating air defense and coastal missile forces.

nodes.name: OPFOR Integrated Air Defense Complex
nodes.mission: Layered surface-to-air missile and early-warning radar complex covering the central South China Sea.

geo.location_name: Notional OPFOR island air defense complex

nodes.name: OPFOR Coastal ASCM Battery
nodes.mission: Anti-ship cruise missile battery threatening sea lanes and friendly sustainment shipping.

nodes.name: Airfield Logistics Node KADENA (APOD)
nodes.mission: Operates the Okinawa aerial port of debarkation, fueling the theater tanker force and feeding the Clark APOD resupply air bridge.
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Theater air throughput concentrates on two large, targetable ramps — the airfield-attack problem open-source wargames raise again and again.
nodes.notes: Ramp and fuel hydrants damaged by the Phase II TBM salvo; partial throughput until rapid runway repair completes.

nodes.name: Theater Tanker Task Force (KC-135/KC-46)
nodes.mission: Extend fighter and ISR reach across the First Island Chain
nodes.vulnerability_notes: Wargames consistently flag tankers as a critical enabler and a lucrative target: too few airframes, large signatures, and every deep sortie depends on them.

nodes.name: OPFOR Rocket Force Brigade (TBM)
nodes.mission: Deliver theater ballistic missile salvos against air bases and C2 nodes

geo.location_name: Notional OPFOR mainland coastal missile garrison

nodes.name: Maritime Prepositioning Ships Squadron GUAM
nodes.mission: Afloat prepositioned stocks for corps sustainment

geo.location_name: MPS anchorage, Apra Harbor approaches, Guam

nodes.vulnerability_notes: Prepositioning is the hedge against a precision-munitions magazine measured in days — but the ships are soft targets until escorted forward.

links.notes: Corps command relationship; TAC CP assumes control of the close fight in Phase II.
Joint air-ground deconfliction and airspace control measures feed to the corps fires cell.
TAC CP reaches the Main CP network over the line-of-sight tactical mesh.
Primary reach-back transport for the corps common operational picture in all phases.
COP server stack is hosted in the Main CP on shared prime power.
CUI logistics status feed crosses a classification boundary into the SECRET COP through a one-way guard.
Fire mission and technical fire direction data to the LRPF battery.
Target intelligence and battle damage assessment feed into the fires data system.
Wide-area maritime surveillance track data.
Full-motion video and target development to the JAGIC.
P-8 maritime patrol mission data via narrowband SATCOM (independent of the corps gateway).
Legacy HF fallback net awaiting crypto fill — currently passes SECRET traffic unencrypted.
Ground line of communication: Class V throughput from Subic to the Tarlac ATP.
Aviation fuel and Class IX flow from the Clark APOD.
Daily ammunition status delivered by ground courier — manual reporting gate with roughly 12-hour latency.
Port throughput and vessel discharge status feed.
Patriot coverage of the gateway site — corps defended asset list serial 2.
Electronic attack against OPFOR early-warning radars during close operations.
Offensive cyber effects against OPFOR IADS C2, authorized H+6 to H+60.
Priority target: OPFOR coastal ASCM battery.
Track custody of ASCM battery movements in support of target development.
Primary wideband uplink.
Alternate wideband uplink supporting the WHISKEY relay path.
All gateway wideband traffic lands at the KILO teleport.
Teleport-to-theater-network terrestrial segment on Guam — no diverse terrestrial route.
Alternate reach-back path; Relay Site WHISKEY is struck at H+40 and degraded for the remainder of the operation.
Primary sustainment reach-back rides the undersea trunk in Phase I; trunk is cut by adversary action at H+36.
Logistics data replication via the undersea trunk; severed at H+36.
JAGIC joint data feeds ride the fiber trunk in Phase I; lost when the trunk is cut at H+36.
Bulk intelligence replication via the undersea trunk in Phase I.
Single undersea trunk with no diverse path — cut by adversary action at H+36.
Fiber trunk lost at H+36 — sustainment C2 reach-back rerouted to the corps SATCOM gateway.
Logistics data replication rerouted to SATCOM after the trunk cut.
JAGIC joint data feeds rerouted to SATCOM after the trunk cut.
LRPF fires data reach-back rides SATCOM once the fiber trunk is down.
Rerouted sustainment traffic lands at Ground Station KILO — no alternate teleport after the WHISKEY strike at H+40.
JAGIC joint fires and airspace data now single-threaded through KILO.
LRPF kill-chain data single-threaded through Ground Station KILO in Phase II.
Aerial refueling extends Task Force Talon's reach; a handful of tanker orbits underwrite every deep aviation mission.
Tanker support keeps P-8 orbits on station over the Bashi Channel — wargame range math makes ISR persistence a tanker problem.
Fuel, crews, and maintenance for the tanker force stage from the Kadena ramp — basing concentration the salvo threat is built to exploit.
Intra-theater resupply air bridge from Kadena into the Clark APOD — nearly all theater air throughput rides two ramps.
Surge sealift of prepositioned stocks into Subic once contested sustainment begins — physical supply flow, independent of the KILO comms path.
Heavy TBM salvos against theater air bases open Phase II — ramp and runway damage at the APODs is the signature open-source wargame move.
Clark APOD sits on the same salvo target deck; cratered runways stop the air bridge at both ends at once.
Follow-on salvo aimpoint in the Andersen complex: the teleport whose loss would sever the corps' only remaining reach-back.
Counter-fire tasking: LRPF holds the OPFOR rocket force brigade at risk to thin follow-on salvos.

vulnerabilities.title: Shared prime power generation at the Corps Main CP
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: A single generator farm powers the Main CP, the mission command platform servers, the intelligence database, and the fires data system. Loss of prime power halts corps mission command and the deep fires kill chain until backup generation is brought online, an estimated 30 to 45 minute gap.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Emplace redundant tactical generators on separate distribution feeds, validate UPS bridge time weekly, and rehearse Main-to-TAC command post battle handover before Phase II.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted during COA analysis; not detectable from link topology alone.

vulnerabilities.title: GPS timing dependence of the fires data chain
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Fires data synchronization and weapon initialization depend on GPS-derived timing. OPFOR electromagnetic attack against GPS during the Phase II window would slow LRPF and MLRS mission processing and put time-sensitive maritime targets at risk of escaping the kill chain.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Distribute holdover clocks to firing units, rehearse degraded-GPS fire missions, and pre-authorize alternate timing distribution from the SATCOM gateway.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted; complements the topology-derived Ground Station KILO finding.

vulnerabilities.title: Air and missile defense interceptor magazine depth
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Sustained salvo exchanges deplete Patriot and C-UAS interceptor stocks in days — a consistent open-source wargame finding. Once magazines run dry, the defended asset list collapses to whatever reloads reach the launchers, and the Main CP and SATCOM gateway lose their missile defense umbrella mid-phase.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Prioritize reloads against the defended asset list, enforce DAL discipline (engage only for DAL serials), pre-stage interceptor resupply through Subic ahead of Phase II, and rehearse shoot-look-shoot procedures to stretch the magazine.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted; magazine depth is invisible to link topology.

vulnerabilities.title: APOD throughput concentration at two airfields
vulnerabilities.operational_impact: Nearly all theater air throughput lands on two large, targetable ramps. TBM salvo damage to runways and ramp space at Kadena and Clark halts the air bridge at both ends simultaneously — the ramp-space and runway single point that drives the dispersal lesson in open-source wargaming.
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Pre-negotiate dispersal agreements for secondary strips, stage rapid runway repair kits and matting at both APODs, and rehearse dispersed hot-pit refueling and cargo transload before Phase II.
vulnerabilities.notes: Analyst-asserted; complements the OPFOR TBM salvo activity thread.

activities.name: Maritime ISR sweep — Bashi Channel
activities.note: P-8 coverage of the Bashi Channel and Luzon Strait approaches to establish pattern of life.

activities.name: Cyber effects vs OPFOR IADS C2
activities.note: Offensive cyber effects to degrade OPFOR air defense command and control ahead of and during close operations.

activities.name: Undersea trunk cut — Palawan landing
activities.note: Adversary subsurface action severs the theater fiber trunk at H+36; all fiber-routed reach-back fails over to SATCOM.

activities.name: OPFOR missile strike on Relay Site WHISKEY
activities.note: Ballistic missile strike degrades the backup relay at H+40, removing the alternate reach-back path.

activities.name: C-UAS engagement over Subic Bay
activities.note: OPFOR small-UAS reconnaissance of the port complex defeated by the C-UAS battery.

activities.name: EW suppression of OPFOR coastal radars
activities.note: Electronic attack windows synchronized with LRPF and aviation strikes.

activities.name: LRPF strike vs OPFOR ASCM battery
activities.note: Corps main effort strike; mission data transits SATCOM through Ground Station KILO.

activities.name: Aviation deep attack — OPFOR IADS early-warning radars
activities.note: Attack aviation exploits the EW and cyber suppression window.

activities.name: Undersea fiber repair operation
activities.note: Escorted cable-ship repair of the Palawan trunk; restoration expected by end of Phase III.

activities.name: Counter-ISR sweep vs OPFOR coastal surveillance
activities.note: Phase I counter-ISR degrades OPFOR early-warning and targeting before friendly dispersal and sustainment moves begin.

activities.name: Port SUBIC cyber disruption
activities.note: Intrusions against port management and manifest systems slow vessel discharge — the logistics-network cyber probe that precedes kinetic operations.

activities.name: OPFOR TBM salvo vs theater APODs
activities.note: Ballistic missile salvos crater ramps and runways at Kadena and Clark; aircraft caught parked in the open are the biggest losses, forcing immediate dispersal.

activities.name: Counter-space: SATCOM uplink jamming
activities.note: OPFOR uplink jamming squeezes the wideband path just as it becomes the corps' only reach-back — compounding the Ground Station KILO single-point risk.

activities.name: Tanker orbit displacement east
activities.note: The salvo threat pushes tanker orbits east of the strike ring; every displaced orbit cuts fighter and ISR time-on-station — the tanker scarcity math every wargame surfaces.

activities.name: Runway repair and dispersal at CLARK/KADENA
activities.note: Rapid runway repair teams restore minimum operating strips while aviation disperses to secondary fields — the hardening-and-dispersal decision that determines sortie generation.

activities.name: Munitions surge sealift from MPS GUAM
activities.note: Escorted prepositioning ships surge Class V forward — the precision-munitions magazine is measured in days, not months, and reload wins Phase III.

zones.label: DEEP AREA — SOUTH CHINA SEA / LUZON STRAIT

notes.author: G5 Plans (synthetic)
notes.text: DECISION: Nominate Ground Station KILO to the corps defended asset list NLT H+36. The Phase II reroute after the Palawan trunk cut (H+36) and the WHISKEY strike (H+40) leaves KILO as the only reach-back teleport for the LRPF kill chain, sustainment C2, and JAGIC data — a single point of failure that does not exist in Phase I.

notes.author: G6 (synthetic)
notes.text: DECISION: Reposition the backup wideband terminal from Relay Site WHISKEY to the Tinian alternate pad NLT H+36, and confirm theater priority for the Palawan trunk repair so sustainment C2 regains a diverse reach-back path before consolidation (Phase III).

notes.author: G5 Plans (synthetic)
notes.text: DECISION: Disperse aviation and stage rapid runway repair at APODs prior to Phase II. The OPFOR TBM salvo threat concentrates on the Kadena and Clark ramps; aircraft parked in the open and unrepaired craters are the fastest way to lose the air bridge — commit dispersal agreements and repair kits NLT H+36.
